question: [File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: '] [Function: getOpenApiMap()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the permanent caching of `openApiMap` to cause all API requests to use incorrect default parameter values if the map is initialized during a transient error state (e.g., partially loaded spec), since the map is never re-initialized after the first call?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: '] [Function: serveSwaggerDocs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Swagger UI's `explorer: false` setting — which disables the URL input box — to note that the spec URL is still directly accessible and use it to download the spec without the UI, bypassing any UI-level restrictions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: '] [Function: getOpenApiParameters()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `p.$ref?.includes(OPEN_API_PARAMETER_LOCATION)` filter in `getOpenApiParameters()` to bypass the filter by crafting a `$ref` that includes the string `#/components/parameters/` as a substring but points to a different location (e.g., `http://evil.com/#/components/parameters/foo`), causing an external reference to be resolved?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: '] [Function: openApiValidator()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `ignoreUndocumented: true` option to send requests to paths that differ from documented paths only by trailing slashes or case differences (e.g., `/api/v1/Accounts` vs `/api/v1/accounts`), bypassing validation on the undocumented variant?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: '] [Function: serveSpec()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `serveSpec` endpoint to perform information disclosure by reading the OpenAPI spec's `info.contact`, `info.license`, and `servers` fields, revealing internal server URLs, contact emails, or infrastructure details?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: '] [Function: getV1OpenApiFile()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a time-of-check-time-of-use (TOCTOU) race condition between the `isUndefined(v1OpenApiFile)` check and the `fs.readFileSync()` call in `getV1OpenApiFile()`, causing two concurrent requests to both read the file simultaneously and potentially cache different versions of the spec?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: '] [Function: serveSwaggerDocs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Swagger UI endpoint to perform a reflected XSS attack by passing malicious query parameters (e.g., `?url=javascript:alert(1)`) that swagger-ui-express processes and reflects in the rendered HTML without sanitization?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: '] [Function: getOpenApiParameters()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `defaultValue = '' + defaultValue` coercion in `getOpenApiParameters()` to cause a very large string to be stored as a default value if the YAML spec contains a large numeric or object default, consuming excessive memory when the `openApiMap` is populated?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: '] [Function: openApiValidator()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the dynamic `await import('express-openapi-validator')` in `openApiValidator()` to cause a module resolution failure if the package is not installed, resulting in an unhandled promise rejection that crashes the server during startup?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: '] [Function: serveSwaggerDocs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `app.use()` registration of `swaggerUi.serve` at the Swagger UI path to cause path confusion attacks where requests to `/api/v1/docs/../other-path` are served by the swagger-ui static middleware instead of the intended route handler?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: '] [Function: getSpecPath()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of input sanitization on `config.openapi.specFileName` in `getSpecPath()` to inject URL-encoded path separators (`%2F`) that are decoded by the file system layer, causing path traversal to read arbitrary files?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: '] [Function: serveSpec()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `serveSpec` endpoint to perform a denial-of-service by sending a large number of concurrent requests, since each request calls `getV1OpenApiFile()` which — after the first call — returns the cached string, but the HTTP response serialization still consumes bandwidth and CPU for each request?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: '] [Function: getOpenApiMap()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `map.set(path, parameters)` call in `getOpenApiMap()` where `path` is a key from `openApiObject.paths`, to cause prototype pollution if the OpenAPI spec contains a path key of `__proto__` or `constructor`, polluting the Map's prototype?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: '] [Function: serveSwaggerDocs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Swagger UI to perform CSRF attacks by using the interactive UI to trigger state-changing API calls from a victim's browser session, since the Swagger UI is served without CSRF protection and makes direct API calls?
